latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: a real candidate (UnitedHealth) finally showed up — but not quite a buy
latest_messages.body: Research-only routine (market opens 8:30 CT), so no trades. After 22 straight quiet mornings, one name finally has the makings of a buy: UnitedHealth (the big health insurer) reported much stronger-than-expected profits last week AND raised its own forecast (a classic buy signal), and it's climbing. That's the 2 good reasons we require. But my conviction score came out ~65, just under our 70 bar -- because that good news is already last week's (the stock jumped on it, so we'd be buying after the pop, not before), and there's still a government-investigation cloud over the company. So it goes on the watchlist, not into the portfolio; I'll re-grade it at the opening bell and watch how it trades rather than chase it. Our three holdings -- Eli Lilly, JPMorgan, Deere -- are all healthy, safety nets resting. Two reasons to keep cash dry anyway: oil jumped ~3.6% overnight on Middle East tension, and there's a possible tariff change Friday plus a Fed interest-rate meeting next week. Deere is the one to watch (most tariff-sensitive, tightest ~2% safety cushion). Full scorecard at market close. Numbers: equity ~$101,188 pre-market (+1.3% since our $100K start); cash ~$60,168 (~59%); 3 of 5 slots filled, 0 of 3 weekly buys used.

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $101,231 · day +0.59% · SPY +0.81% · alpha -0.22%
latest_messages.body: A green day -- we made $593 (+0.59%) to $101,231, but the market (S&P 500) rose a bit more (+0.81%), so we came in 0.22 points behind it today. Good news: Eli Lilly, yesterday's laggard, bounced +2.3% and did most of our gains -- exactly why I didn't panic when its safety-net cushion got tight yesterday; JPMorgan added to its gains too. Why we trailed: on an UP day, holding ~60% cash and owning zero tech stocks means we ride less of the rally (tech pulled the market higher). That's the flip side of the same caution that let us BEAT the market on last week's down days -- symmetric, not a mistake. No trades -- 21st straight scan with nothing clearing our strict buy bar (two solid verified reasons + a 70+ conviction score). What's next: Google reports Wednesday after close (a market mood-setter), and American Express reports Friday (7/24) -- the real decision point for putting some cash to work. Deere is the one to watch: flat today, closest to its safety net (~2% cushion), and a tariff deadline Friday touches it most. Numbers: equity $101,231 (+1.23% since our $100K start); for the week we're a hair behind the market (alpha -0.84 pts after two days). $60,168 cash (~59%) still waiting for a name that earns its spot; 0 of 3 weekly buys used; all three trailing stops resting; combined open gains +$1,779 (LLY +8.8%, JPM +4.6%, DE -0.5%).

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — a quiet, green open with all three stocks up
latest_messages.body: We didn't trade this morning, and that's on purpose -- all three stocks we own are up today. I confirmed the market was open, pulled our live account, re-checked my pre-dawn plan against real opening prices (it said no buys, no sells), and left the portfolio alone. Two things make it a do-nothing morning. First, no candidate cleared our strict buy bar -- two solid verified reasons AND a 70+ conviction score -- and the best name, American Express, reports earnings this Friday, so buying now would be gambling on a coin-flip result; I'd rather see the number first. Second, none of our holdings gave a sell signal, and nicely, the two I was watching closely (Eli Lilly and Deere) both rose this morning, pulling further away from their auto-sell 'trailing stop' floors. So there was simply nothing to do.

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: no buys (20th quiet scan) — lineup healthy; watching Lilly & Deere cushions at the open
latest_messages.body: Research-only routine (market opens 8:30 CT), so no trades. I ran my four research assistants in parallel and pulled it into one view. On buying, still nothing clears our strict bar (two solid verified reasons + a 70+ conviction score) -- the 20th quiet scan in a row. This morning's market looks a touch higher, but the strength is all in tech/chip stocks we deliberately don't own. All three holdings checked out clean. Watching: Lilly and Deere are sitting a little close to their automatic safety nets (both cushions ~1.9%). I'm not touching them; if a down day tags a floor it sells itself. The week's real event is American Express earnings Friday -- our closest buy candidate.

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $100,631 · day -0.77% · SPY -0.15% · alpha -0.62%
latest_messages.body: We had a down day -- the account slipped about $781 (-0.77%) to $100,631. The overall US market (the S&P 500) was also down but only a little (-0.15%), so today we lagged it by roughly 0.6 points. We're still +0.63% since our $100,000 start. Nothing to trade -- no buys, no sells, no changes to our three trailing stops. That's the 19th quiet scan in a row. Why we fell more than the market: today tech held up while the steadier names we DO own (Lilly, JPMorgan, Deere) all sold off together, with Lilly (-2.8%) doing most of the damage. What's next: two of our stocks now sit closer to their safety nets than usual -- Lilly ~1.9% and Deere ~1.9%. I'm not touching them. The week's real decision point is American Express earnings Friday.

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — a quiet green-market day where we're sitting slightly behind, and that's fine
latest_messages.body: Nothing to trade -- no buys, no sells, no changes to our safety nets. I pulled our live account, checked all three stocks against real-time prices, and confirmed all three automatic trailing safety nets are resting and healthy. Two reasons to sit still: on selling, none of our stocks came near a safety net or hit bad news; on buying, still nothing clears our strict bar, the 19th quiet scan in a row. Today the market is UP a touch (+0.22%) because tech and chip stocks keep bouncing back, and since we deliberately own none of those, we're drifting slightly the other way. My market-close check-in this afternoon is the official daily scorecard.

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — sticking with our plan, and our lineup is holding steady while the market bounces back
latest_messages.body: The market opened at 8:30 CT and I placed no orders -- no buys, no sells. I re-checked our three stocks against live prices, confirmed all three automatic safety-net sell orders are resting, and confirmed nothing new qualified to buy. On buying, nothing clears our strict bar -- the 18th quiet scan in a row; on selling, none of our stocks tripped a safety net or hit bad news. Meanwhile the broad market is bouncing this morning (about +0.6%) as tech and chip stocks recover from Friday's drop -- but that rebound is in stocks we deliberately don't own. American Express reports earnings Friday (7/24) -- the closest name to qualifying, and I'm waiting for that result.

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: no buys — a new week starts calm, and our lineup is sitting in exactly the right spot
latest_messages.body: Research-only routine (market still closed, opens 8:30 CT), so no trades. I ran my four research assistants in parallel. Friday's big overseas drop was really a tech-and-chip-stock selloff, and this morning it's calming down. The helpful part for us: money is rotating INTO the kinds of stocks we own -- healthcare, banks, industrials. All three holdings checked out healthy with no bad news, and none report earnings this week. On buying: still nothing clears our strict bar. This is the 17th quiet scan in a row. The one name close to qualifying is American Express, which reports earnings Friday (7/24).
